name: Gremlin Chaos Engineering API
what_it_does: Enterprise chaos engineering platform with REST API — injecting controlled failures (CPU, memory, network, disk, time) into infrastructure and applications to validate reliability, identify weaknesses, and build confidence in system resilience before production incidents occur.
use_cases: Running chaos experiments programmatically via API as part of CI/CD reliability gates
Scheduling automated game days with CPU, network, and container failure attacks
Querying experiment results and system health data for reliability reporting
Automating failover scenario validation for disaster recovery testing
Integrating chaos results with incident management tools via webhook
not_for: Load testing and performance benchmarking (use k6 or Locust)
Functional test automation (use Playwright or Cypress)
Production monitoring and observability (use Datadog or Prometheus)
best_when: An SRE or platform team wants enterprise chaos engineering with safe guardrails, attack scheduling, and API integration to validate system resilience systematically.
avoid_when: You need load testing, functional testing, or are not ready for controlled failure injection in your infrastructure.

auth.notes: API key (team key) from Gremlin console. Bearer token auth. Team-scoped keys. Key/secret pair for authentication. Scoped for attack execution vs read-only reporting. Webhook for experiment events.

pricing.notes: Free tier for basic chaos experiments. Enterprise pricing for production use. US-based San Francisco company. Founded 2016 — pioneers of enterprise chaos engineering. Netflix and AWS alumni founders. Kubernetes, ECS, Bare metal support. Strong SRE community. Steady state hypothesis framework.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per plan; documented in API reference

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for attacks, scenarios, schedules, and reports. Key/secret pair auth. Webhook for attack events. Good documentation. Attack types: resource (CPU, memory), state (shutdown, time travel), network (blackhole, latency), disk. Halt attack via API for safety. Agents must be installed on targets.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Attack creation creates new attack jobs — not idempotent. Attack halting is idempotent. Scenario management idempotent by ID. Report reads idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Gremlin agent must be installed on target hosts before attacks can run
Key/secret pair auth (not standard Bearer — uses key:secret base64 encoding)
Always implement halt mechanism before running automated attacks
Attack radius controls (blast radius) must be configured for safety

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. SOC2 certified. US data residency. Key/secret pair auth with scope separation. Agent-based architecture — attacks run locally, not from Gremlin cloud. Blast radius controls for safety. Enterprise-grade security for failure injection platform.
